lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Hello there, I'm busy with my research. Come back in a bit, could you?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Hello there and welcome to my humble abode. It's sadly rather more humble than I'd like, to be honest, although perhaps you can help with that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would be happy to make your home a better place.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Yay, I didn't even have to talk about a reward; you're more gullible than most adventurers, that's for sure.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Don't worry, though; I just need you to help fix this vile old bed for me. Then I'll find a suitable reward for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, will I be able to take a nap in it?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Don't be silly; everyone knows that true warriors don't ever sleep...or perform many other bodily functions, for that matter. I'll come up with something, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not convinced by just a vague something; can you be a slight bit more inspiring in your offer?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: What I need is simple: a couple of undead chickens. You should be able to pick some up at the farm near Port Phasmatys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well then, I shall await my mystery prize with bated breath.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not much into interior design, to tell the truth.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Very well, I suppose I'll just have to resort to the Professor's patented sleeping aid in order to get some rest. It does so bruise my head, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a nice girl like you doing in a place like this?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I'm not a nice girl. I'm a respectable scientist researching the interactions between ghosts and the force of pure science.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I took over the position of assistant the the Professor upstairs, although, to be honest, his methods are somewhat eccentric. Did you head about the Ernest scandal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, yes. I helped sort that out; he did seem a bit odd.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You don't know the half of it - can you believe that he thinks he has proven Goldbach's First Conjecture using an optimised segmented sieve?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, quite, Yes.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Do you need a reminder what you are supposed to do? I know you must have shiny beads distracting you, but it is an important job you are doing for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: ...And people say we scientists are absent minded...

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You need to fetch 2 undead chickens for me to use in my redecoration. It's not rocket science.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rocket?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: If they existed, they would be like arrows but much, much bigger. Considering the mess you rangers make with small bits of stick, we scientists have decided they don't exist. Now get chicken hunting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you; I just came to gossip with you.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Well, the Witch next door has been cooking some rather nice gingerbread, but I don't think that counts as gossip.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Can you head off and do adventurous stuff, please? I hear the Wilderness is fun this time of year.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: What are you doing? I'm not ticklish.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: What are you doing? Yuck, dead things.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: What are you doing? Do I look that thin?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: What are you doing? Trying to infect me with some vile disease?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I'm not sure what you are trying to do, are you sure you aren't slightly deranged?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about a quest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am after one of your, er, unhealthier poultry. Could you help me?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: You need those useless, undead chickens? How odd you adventurers are.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: You need to talk to my husband, though - not that I can these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whyever would this be?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Can't you see, he is dead. I can't talk to the dead.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Woo woo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your animals don't look too healthy.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: It's that fountain thingy in the temple to the east. It's turned them all into zombies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What use are zombie animals?

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: None at all, mate, except that those worshippers at that temple keep comin' and killin' 'em all for their bones. Don't ask me why.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're a ghost - surely you know something about it.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: I don't know nuthin' about nuthin'. Oim a simple ghost with simple needs. All I know is, years ago, that temple started glowing green and, a few months later, I woke up dead. That's all there is to it.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: I do miss the wife though; tell 'er I still loves her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would I be able to buy some of your chickens?

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Talk to my wife and I'll think about it.

sections.title: Talking to Malcolm again before talking to Alice

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: 'Ave you talked to my wife for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet; I've been distracted by the thought of undead cow milk.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about a quest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a message from your husband. He wants you to know that he still loves you, despite his ghostly state.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: The curse of undeath was so cruel; all the men out here succumbed, but Lyra and I were left alive.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Tell him I love him but I can't find our savings. I know he had our collection of gold and 'prize cow' rosettes just before the curse struck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have a word with him, then; magic has its uses I suppose.

sections.title: Talking to Alice again before talking to her husband again

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Have you spoken to my husband yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your wife says she needs the family cash and wants to know what you did with it.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Tell 'er I spent it on cheap spirits, har har.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your sense of humour died too, it seems...

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Hah, just trying to lift your spirits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I rest my case.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Suit yerself, stick-in-the-mud. Anyway, Oim not one o' them yokels. Tell 'er I putted the cash in the bank like she always told me to.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: A warning to ya, too: annoy her and I'll haunt ya till yer hair turns white.

sections.title: Talking to Malcolm before talking to Alice again

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Any luck wiv me wife?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing new, no.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about that quest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your husband says he put the cash in the bank.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: I'll need his bank pass, in that case.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't you just take a ghostspeak amulet? Then you could talk to him directly.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: I tried that once, but all those other ghosts - and even the undead chickens and cows - scared me so much. I wouldn't try it again for all the cash in Varrock bank.

sections.title: Talking to Alice again before talking to her husband again

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Have you asked him about the bank pass?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You may not believe me, but she wants me to find your bank pass now.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Maybe she said that, maybe she didn't. I think you're just after me savings. Tell 'er that no one but a fool gives away their pass numbers.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Go tell 'er now, if you're not a double-dealin' scammer, that is.

sections.title: Talking to Malcolm again before talking to Alice again

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: 'Ave you talked to 'er?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not since we last spoke.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about that quest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He says he won't trust me with the bank pass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I gave some sort of altered ghostspeak amulet to him - surely that would work?

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: You're so clever; I've overheard passing adventurers say that there's some witch near here who changes ghostspeak amulets.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: I think she lives a bit west of that mad Professor Fenksomething, past the Farming patch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see if I can find her. Big nose and a monstrous hat, I assume? I wonder where the beautiful young witches hide...

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Mysterious indeed, but in this case she actually looks pretty normal.

sections.title: Talking to Alice before obtaining the amulet

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Have you found a way for me to talk to my husband yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've not progressed at all, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the farmers east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alice and her husband are having trouble talking to one another and said you might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Ah, I know them; shame about those cows. Why would they think that I could help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alice seems to think you could alter a ghostspeak amulet in order to allow them to communicate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In addition, when I was attempting to thwart Necrovarus, you created a new type of amulet for me. I thought that perhaps you might also do so on this occasion.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Well, the poor young lady has such family problems; I quite feel her pain. I'd be happy to help.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: You seem to have one of her golden hairs on your shoulder, so I can use that...

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Talk to me again with a ghostspeak amulet and some space in your backpack and I'll be ready to work on this little good deed. The way I plan is quite simple, really.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I can mirror part of the unused mystical essence of the ghostspeak amulet, bind it with Alice's hair and thus create a second amulet.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: The second amulet will be useful for the purpose you desire, though it won't work for any other ghost or human other than the farmer and his wife.

sections.title: Speaking to the old crone without a ghostspeak amulet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the farmers east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to see if you are ready to do your mystical stuff with my ghostspeak amulet.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I most certainly am, but you don't have an ghostspeak amulet.

sections.title: Speaking to the old crone without inventory space

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the farmers east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to see if you are ready to do your mystical stuff with my ghostspeak amulet.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I most certainly am, but you don't have space in your backpack.

sections.title: Speaking to the old crone again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the farmers east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to see if you are ready to do your mystical stuff with my ghostspeak amulet.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I most certainly am; there you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that was quick and painless.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Just being a good neighbour.

sections.title: Speaking to the old crone after losing the ghostspeak amulet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the farmers east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you be able to replace the amulet you made? I seem to have lost it.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Here you are; lucklily, I saved some of Alice's hair in case you were careless. Which you were.

sections.title: Speaking to the old crone before delivering the amulet to Alice

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the farmers east of here.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, to tell the truth, I just came back to chat with you. Any news?

lines.speaker: Old Crone
lines.text: Disgraceful! Deliver that amulet; a young lady's happiness depends upon it.

sections.title: Trying to equip the Crone-made amulet

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Have you handed him an enhanced amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have obtained the amulet; I just haven't handed it over yet. So, it's looking good!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I talked to your wife and thought that if you had a special amulet, you could speak to her and sort out the bank situation without me being involved.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Arr, that makes far more sense than I was expecting from a muscle-head like you. My wife's a clever one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... oh, never mind. I'm desperate enough for those chickens to let that pass.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Give me that amulet, then, and we'll be seeing about your unnatural desire for chickens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, you need it more than I do, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Ta, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lucky we had such a brilliant idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I'm ready to hand it over yet.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Ahhh, many thanks. Now what was it you were wanting again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a couple of your chickens.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Chickens is tricksy, 'specially dead 'uns. I'll have to catch 'em for ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They look pretty pathetic; how hard can it be?

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Stand back while I catches 'em, ya city slicker.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Here, chicky chicky!

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Git 'ere, yer pesky bird!

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Where'd she go?

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Git orf my laaand!

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: You heard my husband: leave now!

lines.speaker: Cow31337Killer
lines.text: Always the same; I can never get these animals to myself.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: You killed Bessie!

lines.speaker: Cow31337Killer
lines.text: Buying cowhides and feathers - ahh, that chicken is next; feathers for me!

lines.speaker: Sneaky undead fowl
lines.text: Woo woo woo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's one way to catch a chicken, I suppose.

sections.title: Speaking to Malcolm after catching the chicken

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Hello, how can I help you? I'm sellin' if you have ecto- tokens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I buy those chickens now?

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: I can hand over a chicken if you give me 10 of them ecto-token thingies per bird.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Ta, some day soon I'll be havin' a nice undead bed to kip in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I buy 2 chickens?

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Great! I'm laying away me tokens for some killer cows. That'll learn them bone rustlers.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: Har har, how many ghost chicky farmers d'you know, den?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your animals don't look too healthy.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: It's that fountain thingy in the temple to the east. It's turned them all into zombies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What use are zombie animals?

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: None at all, mate, except that those worshippers at that temple keep comin' and killin' 'em all for their bones. Don't ask me why.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're a ghost - surely you know something about it.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: I don't know nuthin' about nuthin'. Oim a simple ghost with simple needs. All I know is, years ago, that temple started glowing green and, a few months later, I woke up dead. That's all there is to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm okay, thank you.

lines.speaker: Malcolm
lines.text: The ghosts I talk to say that the tokens have something to do with the tower just east of here. If you need to collect some I'd try there.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Hello, friend, and thanks for allowing me to talk to my husband Malcolm. How can I help you?

sections.title: Speaking to Ava without undead chickens

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: My spiritometric devices show that you have been in close contact with ghostly animals. Are we closer to success?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I can buy the ones you want but I don't have any on me.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Stunning. I should have tried to hire some gnomes instead. They may be obsessed with their bellies but they can at least be trusted to deliver unpleasant perishables.

sections.title: Speaking to Ava while wielding an undead chicken

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: My spiritometric devices show that you have been in close contact with ghostly animals. Are we closer to success?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have this one which I can use to club people.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Are you going to hand it over or do you want to continue to waft it about menacingly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I prefer to keep this fine weapon with which to smite mine enemies.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You would be more menacing if your weapon weren't quite so rotten.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can take it; it smells anyway.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: On second thought, the way you wave it about makes me fear for my life. Stow it in your back and talk to me again.

sections.title: Speaking to Ava with one undead chicken

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: My spiritometric devices show that you have been in close contact with ghostly animals. Are we closer to success?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's one.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Thanks! This is great for me, although I'll need another one if you want any sort of reward.

sections.title: Speaking to Ava before giving her another chicken

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Why do you come back with no chickens? I'll be needing another one before I give you your reward! I'm not going to relent, even if you say 'plz plz plz' and claim you were just scammed.

sections.title: Handing over the second chicken to Ava

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's the second one for my reward.

sections.title: Talking to Ava again before talking to the witch

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Go and talk to the Witch next door. She'd talk the hid legs off a donkey but she can select the iron with which is suitable for the chicken to interact.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Despite my extensive studies, her years of experience make her better at instinctive magico-mystical interaction. Oh well, at least I'm cleverer, prettier and will have a better bed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, you're great. Yes, I'll go and talk to her when you've finished praising yourself.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: My spiritometric devices show that you have been in close contact with ghostly animals. Are we closer to success?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here they are.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Amazing! Success! I can look forward to some good nights' sleep after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I ask exactly how an undead chicken will help you sleep?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Well, I need the feathers to make my bed more comfortable. A comfortable bed will help me sleep. Obvious, really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Obvious, yes, but why on Gielinor would you need an undead chicken when there are perfectly good live chickens just down the road?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Well, for a start, undead feathers are much cleaner than living ones; no dust mites or anything. Secondly, I always think of Ernest when I see a chicken, so my nerves can't take killing them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why do I need a chicken for my reward; we already established that I don't use a bed?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Seeing as how you ranger types use so many feathers in your arrows, I was thinking I could harness an undead chicken to make an unending supply of arrow flights for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Beats chicken slaying or hanging around in fishing shops, I suppose. So, what next?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: We'll need a magnet next, one with purely natural fields and made from a carefully selected iron bar. A firm impact when the iron is parallel to Gielinor's field will stabilise this field in the rod.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Go and talk to the Witch next door.

sections.title: Talking to the Witch again without iron bars

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am back.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Oh, but sugarpie, I need 5 iron bars, you don't have any. Come back to me quickly with all 5 of them.

sections.title: Talking to the Witch with less than five iron bars

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some iron bars here.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Oh, but sugarpie, I need 5 iron bars, you don't have enough. Come back to me quickly with all 5 of them.

sections.title: Talking to Ava before giving the iron bars to the Witch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've talked to the Witch and she has asked me for some iron bars.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Do I resemble an iron-bar merchant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, maybe; I've never met one.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: An entirely logical answer; you'll make a scientiest yet.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Great, you'll go far! I made some nice painted metal toys for you, snookums.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Toys? Snookums? What are you on about, you deranged old bat?

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Oh, forget it, then. If you won't react to kindness, I'm back to luring infants into my oven. You'll have it on your conscience.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Go to the iron mine just north-east of Rimmington and hit the bar with a plain old smithing hammer while facing north. Then take your new magnet to Ava. Poor girl, having to deal with whippersnappers like you.

sections.title: Talking to the Witch after losing the selected iron

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: I can't say that I didn't expect this; you'd lose your head if it weren't glued on. Lucky I made some replacements. Take this one and leave me in peace.

sections.title: Talking to the Witch before making the magnet

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: You were sent to try my patience, weren't you? Go away and make that magnet, then hand it to Ava.

sections.title: Talking to Ava before making the magnet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've talked to the Witch and now I've given her some iron bars.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I'm thrilled for you. What about my magnet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The process is subject to some unexpected delays.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I can see now why Fenkenstain prefers to work with dead folk. It can't be more frustrating than working with you.

sections.title: Using hammer on selected iron without being in Rimmington mine
Using hammer on selected iron without facing north
Using hammer on selected iron as intended
Talking to the Witch after creating the magnet

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Hoorah for you, you have the magnet. Just hand it over to Ava. I'm working on my new recipe for spit-roast adventurer, so disturbing me isn't wise.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Hello, hello, my poppet. What brings you to my little room?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ava told me to ask you about making magnets. Something about natural fields and other stuff. Sounded like she needed a farmer, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Don't worry, deary, I can tell you just what to do and you won't have to worry your pretty head about the complicated bits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No need to patronise me quite so much, you know.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: I went to anger management classes, my lambkin; that's why I was treating you so kindly. It's either this way of talking or I'll go back to shoving children into ovens.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Just bring me 5 iron bars, though, and you're well on the way to never having to talk to me again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back.

sections.title: Talking to Ava before chopping at undead trees

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: So, what terrible hitch have you encountered now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing really; I just decided to talk to you in case you have any great advice for me.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Well, I've heard that all kinds of dragons and even basilisks can drop mithril axes, if that's why you are delaying. Most of these creatures are silly enough that you can even hide behind something and plink them to

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: death in the brave ranger way.

sections.title: Trying to chop an undead tree without mithril axe or better (or without carrying an axe)
Trying to chop an undead tree with mithril axe or better

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've manufactured the magnet; here it is.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Great stuff! With the Witch's influence within the magnet, the undead chicken can use this, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: The plan is that the chicken will operate the magnet to attract bits of iron and steel, maybe even your own recently fired arrows. There are plenty of totally lost arrowheads lying about in the fields of Gielinor, I bet.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: In addition, arrows which you fire should be able to be attracted back to your quiver by the cunning avian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I begin to understand your plan. We've covered feathers and arrowheads now; what next?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: We need a source of wood, but one which is spiritually active and can regenerate itself. That will save you some axework in the future.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Try using a woodcutting axe on the pesky trees in the garden here, the ones that attack rather than the really dead ones. They are probably just the sort of thing we could use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They will try to kill me, though, and I can't fight back!

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Now you know how those poor guards feel when you hide behind mushrooms and fences and attack them from afar! Anyway, I reckon you'll need to try a mithril or better axe on the trees. At least the trees are

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: pretty close.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I tried to hack the trees with my axe, but it just bounced off the trunk! It did all seem all-too-convenient to work on the first try.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Fortunately for you, I've done some research and it seems to suggest that there are two choices open to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me the worst.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: The first is more interesting. We cut off your arms, have them reanimated as undead, reattach them and then you should be able to cut the trees normally.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Of course, you won't be able to pick your nose anymore, so I suppose you'll want to try the second option.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not exactly addicted to picking my nose, but I do think I'll pass on that method.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Well, in that case, I think it may have something to do with Slayer abilities. After all, I did see [Turael/Aya] poking around the trees while I was moving in.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: As [he's/she's] not known for [his/her] random touristic activities, you should try chatting with this [Turael/Aya]. [He's/She's] the Slayer Master near Burthorpe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear, I hope [he/she] doesn't want me to buy one of [his/her] ridiculous fashion accessories. Those earmuffs [he/she] sells make heroic adventurers into a laughing stock.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about a quest. Ava said she saw you hanging around the moving trees near Draynor Manor.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Ahh, you came to the right man; odd things, those trees. What is it you are needing exactly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I need some of the wood from them, but my axe just bounced off the trunk.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Sounds like you need a blessed axe. No one really makes them, though, these days.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: Blessed axes are too soft to cut normal wood since the axe's edge must be replaced by holy silver. I can do you a favour, though.

lines.speaker: Turael
lines.text: If you can give me a mithril axe and a holy symbol of Saradomin I can let you have my axe. I'll make myself a new one when no one is pestering me for Slayer tasks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so I'll see whether I can spare an axe and a symbol. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about a quest. Ava said she saw you hanging around the moving trees near Draynor Manor.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: Ahh, you came to the right man; odd things, those trees. What is it you are needing exactly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I need some of the wood from them, but my axe just bounced off the trunk.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: Sounds like you need a blessed axe. No one really makes them, though, these days.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: Blessed axes are too soft to cut normal wood since the axe's edge must be replaced by holy silver. I can do you a favour, though.

lines.speaker: Aya
lines.text: If you can give me a mithril axe and a holy symbol of Saradomin I can let you have my axe. I'll make myself a new one when no one is pestering me for Slayer tasks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so I'll see whether I can spare an axe and a symbol. Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Turael or Aya again

lines.speaker: Turael/Aya
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I'm here about those trees again.

lines.speaker: Turael/Aya
lines.text: I can make an axe for you now, if you wish. Remember, it will be no use for normal woodcutting after I have added the silver edge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd love one, thanks.

lines.speaker: Turael/Aya
lines.text: Here's a new axe; may it serve you well.

lines.speaker: Turael/Aya
lines.text: You'll need to hand over both a mithril axe and holy symbol of Saradomin. You don't have a symbol in your pack, so I'm not able to help.

lines.speaker: Turael/Aya
lines.text: You'll need to hand over both a mithril axe and holy symbol of Saradomin. You don't have an axe in your pack, so I'm not able to help.

lines.speaker: Turael/Aya
lines.text: You'll need to hand over both a mithril axe and holy symbol of Saradomin. You don't have an axe in your pack, so I'm not able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Turael/Aya
lines.text: Goodbye then. You may, of course, return if you decide you want a new axe.

sections.title: Talking to Ava before obtaining a blessed axe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Turael/Aya], the Slayer Master, told me what to do - with no earmuffs, spiky helmets or other laughable headgear involved.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: A pity; you'd look quite cute with rabbit ears. Perhaps I should have bribed [him/her].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You scare me sometimes.

sections.title: Talking to Ava after obtaining a blessed axe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'm ready to cut those trees now.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: What do you want? A prize? Go and get the wood; it's not as if the trees are wandering about and hard to find.

sections.title: Failing to chop an undead tree

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that undead wood at last. Well, twigs anyway.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You certainly took your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd say they didn't grow on trees, but I guess you'd just be sarcastic about my sense of humour.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Quite. Now that we have all the ingredients for infinite arrows, we just need a container in which we can keep the components in the correct mutual alignment.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I've gathered together some research notes from various sources but I can't quite make out what they mean. If you want to have a go at making them out, just ask me for a copy.

sections.title: Talking to Ava after losing the research notes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost the research notes.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Don't tell me, your cat ate them? You won't get out of the job that easily; here are some copies I made.

sections.title: Talking to Ava with the notes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the notes but haven't translated them yet. Any hints?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I know you have the notes; I gave them to you, in case you forgot. Furthermore, if I had hints, I'd have translated those notes myself.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: So, take the hint and go off and translate them. If it's too hard, you can always go and shoot demons in cages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to look at those research notes now, unless you have translated them without me?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: They are still stumping me. Here are the notes; I really hope your head doesn't explode from reading them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd find it slightly inconvenient, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: It wouldn't be all bad as your body would be useful for research after death. What I'd be upset about was if bits of you landed in my nice new bed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your concern is touching.

sections.title: Talking to Ava again with the pattern

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I do with this pattern again?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Your short-term memory loss worries me. Combine the pattern with hard leather and some polished buttons, then hand the resulting container to me.

sections.title: Talking to Ava after losing the pattern

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The pattern seems to have vanished from my pack... not my fault, of course.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You're pretty careless, aren't you? I assume you're the type who leaves a trail of arrows and knives behind them when they train?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Here's a replacement; perhaps if I charged for them, you'd be more careful.

sections.title: Trying to combine buttons and leather without a pattern
Trying to combine buttons with pattern without hard leather
Trying to combine hard leather with pattern without buttons
Talking to Ava having lost the container (or having it in the bank)

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You've made the container, I assume?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, yes, although I seem to have mislaid it.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: It's only the final, vital step in your quest that you decided to leave who-knows-where. Don't worry, I have plenty of spares.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do I sense sarcasm somewhere here?

sections.title: Talking to Ava after she gives you another pattern

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You've made one container; surely you can make another. Is it killing a cow or spotting those bright pink H.A.M. members that you are having troubles with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've translated those notes. See? I'm not just a thuggish moron like you seem to think.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: For all I know, it was pure luck, so don't jump to any conclusions about your mighty intellect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see why you don't have any assistants; you're not exactly easy to work with.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Let's get back to the work we're doing, then. Remember, this is all a favour to you; I could have just decided to fob you off with a feather duster.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I've given you a pattern for the container; you'll need to combine them with some polished buttons and hard leather. Then we're almost done. Good news, eh?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: If you are having trouble finding buttons, I've heard rumours that the H.A.M. society carry this sort of stuff more than most.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? How would you know this strange detail?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I hear they lose their clothes a lot to thieves so they have to make do with shoddy goods. Whatever the reason, they seem to carry buttons about in their pockets.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Wow, great, now the arrow manufacturer is ready for use...there you are! Talk to me if you need more information later.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You are also skilled enough that I'm rewarding you with the upgraded version right away. Congratulations.
